completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Stabilized `zentinel doctest --mutate` into a deterministic, documented mutation-aware doctest report surface. src/doctest/mutation_id.zig derives the two durable identities exactly as docs/DOCTEST_SPEC.md specifies: a dm_ case id and a ds_ survivor ref, each `<prefix> + first_26(lowercase_unpadded_crockford_base32(sha256(canonical_bytes)))` over the same ordered fields (doctest_case_id, mutant_id, operator, doc_file, source_ref, normalized_mutated_diff) differing only by the schema-version namespace line, with normalizeDiff stripping trailing whitespace per line. src/doctest/mutation_experiment.zig adds the stabilized builder stableMutationRun: it is opt-in (error.NotOptedIn unless opted_in), preflights each zig_test doctest and emits a single skipped mutation entry (no mutants) when the case has no behavioral assertion or the normal doctest does not pass, and otherwise emits one case.kind="mutation" entry per documentation mutant with a durable dm_ id, the mutation result status (killed/survived/compile_error/compiler_crash/timeout/skipped/invalid), a closed runner_evidence including failure_kind, and a ds_ survivor_ref only for survived mutants (null for every other status); output is sorted by dm_ id and a separate mutation_summary counts mutation cases. schemas/doctest.report.v1.schema.json gains the additive optional case.mutation ($defs/case_mutation), the mutation_runner_evidence def (required failure_kind), the optional summary.mutation ($defs/mutation_summary), and a widened case status enum with an allOf so kind="mutation" entries require mutation, a ^dm_ id, and a mutation status while ordinary cases keep the doctest status set; the top-level summary still counts only preflight non-mutation entries. The experimental zentinel.doctest.mutation_experiment.v1 report and the doctest report.zig Case model were intentionally left unchanged (so the out-of-scope prototype test and all existing doctest report snapshots stay byte-identical), and the CLI wiring (cli.zig, outside this task's scope) still drives the prototype. Non-goals respected: no AI survivor explanations, no ZIR/AIR doctest mutation, no making public docs mutation-gated by default; src/zir_backend.zig, src/air_backend.zig, src/ai/**, and docs/MUTATOR_SPEC.md were not touched (forbidden). docs/DOCTEST_SPEC.md already specified the dm_/ds_/runner_evidence/summary.mutation contract and now agrees with the schema; src/report.zig and src/mutant_runner.zig needed no change.
tests_added: test/doctest_mutate_stabilization_test.zig: opt-in rejection (error.NotOptedIn); deterministic byte-exact snapshots for a killed and a survived documentation mutant; survived mutant gets a ds_ survivor ref and a dm_ id while non-survived get none; runner_evidence carries failure_kind; repeatability (identical inputs -> identical dm_/ds_ ids); normal doctest failure blocks mutation-aware execution (single skipped entry, no mutants); the doctest.report.v1 schema defines the additive case.mutation/summary.mutation/mutation_runner_evidence (required failure_kind) and widened status enum; dm_/ds_ derivation is deterministic and distinct across namespaces.

completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Implemented deterministic parallel mutant execution without changing report ordering. Added src/worker_pool.zig (deterministic_core): a bounded std.Thread pool that maps work-item indices [0, count) to results by INDEX via an atomic counter (each index claimed exactly once), runs inline for jobs<=1 (conservative default), clamps the worker count to [1, min(count, max_jobs=64)] (effectiveJobs), degrades gracefully on spawn failure (the calling thread drains remaining items), and exposes content-addressed per-mutant workspace path helpers (workspaceRoot/cacheDirIn/outDirIn) so concurrent workers never share a writable workspace, local .zig-cache, or zig-out. Restructured src/run_command.zig run() into three phases: Phase A resolves per-file selection and the unmutated preflight serially through the baseline executor and assembles one Job per mutant; Phase B runs the injected MutantRunner across `--jobs` (overriding normalized run.jobs) workers, collecting results into a pre-allocated slice by index with no arena allocation on worker threads; Phase C builds report entries and result-cache keys in mutant order, then calls the existing report.sortAndAssignDisplayIds so the canonical report order is independent of execution order. Removed the run-time `run.jobs > 1` rejection (RunError.JobsNotSupported and its CLI message) and added `--jobs <n>` to parseArgs (ParseError.InvalidJobs rejects non-positive or non-numeric values; --jobs overrides run.jobs). src/cli.zig drops the JobsNotSupported branch, reports the --jobs diagnostic, and routes per-mutant workspace creation through worker_pool.workspaceRoot; the real adapter's rt.gpa/rt.io are the std.process.Init arena/io documented as threadsafe, so concurrent mutant execution is safe. src/root.zig exports worker_pool. completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added src/mutators/loop_boundary.zig (deterministic_core): the stable loop_boundary mutator, completing the twelve minimum-product mutators. For while loops it reads the condition via the while node data (while_simple -> node_and_node[0]; while_cont and @"while" -> node_and_extra[0]) and, when the condition is a boundary comparison (<, <=, >, >=), swaps the inclusive/exclusive boundary (< <-> <=, > <-> >=) with compiles compile expectation (the swap always type-checks). For for ranges it reads the range end (for_range node_and_opt_node[1], skipping open ranges a..) and, when the end is a plain decimal integer literal, emits paired +1/-1 candidates (i128 math, replacement strings via the collector allocator) with may_fail compile expectation (the bound can overflow the range type or form an invalid range). Infinite loops (while true: the condition is the `true` identifier, not a comparison), non-comparison conditions, open or non-integer range ends, and test bodies are skipped. Candidate order is deterministic via the shared collector's canonical sort. Equality comparisons (==, !=) in while conditions are NOT boundary-swapped (boundary is inclusive/exclusive only). completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added src/mutators/integer_boundary.zig (deterministic_core): the stable integer_literal_boundary mutator. It walks comparison nodes (==, !=, <, <=, >, >=) and, for each operand that is a `.number_literal`, emits two boundary candidates: the literal incremented by one and decremented by one (parsed as i128 to avoid overflow when computing +/-1; replacement strings allocated from the collector allocator). Context is an allowlist: only literals that are direct comparison operands are mutated, so this covers branch and length-check boundaries while protected literals -- version numbers, ABI/mask constants, enum tags, array lengths, and align(...) values -- are never examined because they are not comparison operands. Only plain decimal literals are mutated; hex/binary/octal and underscore/suffix forms are skipped as syntax-local (type inference out of scope). Test bodies are excluded. Compile expectation is may_fail because a +/-1 can overflow the operand's integer type (a documented expected compile error, not an invalid candidate). Candidate order is deterministic via the shared collector's canonical sort, giving a stable order for each paired +1/-1. No ast_backend or mutant model change was needed. Re-exported via zentinel.mutators.integer_boundary; run-pipeline enabling remains deferred (run_command.zig out of scope) and reports need no schema change. It walks `.@"catch"` nodes, takes the catch handler (right-hand side) via tree.nodeData(node).node_and_node[1] over firstToken..lastToken, and replaces it with `unreachable`, preserving the EXACT handler source span. For a `catch |err| handler` form only the handler expression is replaced (the |err| capture is token-determined and excluded from the span). It skips an existing `catch unreachable` (forbidden context) and test bodies, carries may_fail compile expectation (the AST layer cannot prove `unreachable` coerces, and a payload form may produce an unused-capture compile error -- a documented expected outcome, not an invalid candidate), and carries MUTATOR_SPEC equivalent-risk hints. A failing command classifies as compile_error when its failure_kind is compile_error and killed when it is test_failure (I-010); timeout -> timeout; abnormal termination -> compiler_crash, not compile_error/invalid/internal_error (I-021); all-passing commands -> survived; status is derived only from command results and patch validity with a closed classifier_source enum (never AI). Generalized runner.classifyBaseline into runner.classifyCommand(phase) so baseline and mutant command classification share one implementation. No real process spawning or filesystem workspace creation in tests; those providers are injected and wired by the run command. No worker pool or cache (task non-goals).

completion_evidence.implementation_summary: Added deterministic property-generator infrastructure. src/property/generator.zig is a pure splitmix64 seeded stream (Generator.init(seed)/next()/intRange/boolean/bytes) with no wall-clock, OS randomness, or global state, so the same seed reproduces the same sequence byte-for-byte and a recorded failing seed is reproducible. src/property/report.zig is the executable structural validator for the zentinel.pipeline.property_report.v1 contract (docs/PROPERTY_TEST_POLICY.md): validate(std.json.Value) returns the first Violation or .ok, enforcing schema_version, scope (property_required/not_property_required), task_class, the six mandatory invariant categories (Determinism, Stability, Round-trip, Isolation, Monotonicity, Collision resistance), a non-empty explicit integer seed list, a generator summary plus generated_cases count, shrink status (not_triggered/minimized/unsupported), the derived rule that report status is failed iff some property failed (status_mismatch otherwise), the missing-evidence rule that a property_required report with no properties is rejected (empty_property_required), a required skip_reason for not_property_required reports, and the rule that a failed property must carry a non-null counterexample plus a minimized/unsupported shrink status. Both modules are exported via the new src/root.zig `property` namespace. test/support/property.zig adds the forAllU64 helper that drives the seeded stream over a predicate and records the seed, generated case count, pass/fail, and a reproducible first-failing counterexample. test/property_generator_test.zig proves: same seed -> identical 256-draw sequence while a neighbouring seed diverges within 16 draws; the helper records seed/case-count/counterexample deterministically; a fully-populated failed report is accepted while a failed report missing its counterexample (failed_without_counterexample) or whose status disagrees with results (status_mismatch) is rejected; the task-044 high-risk missing-evidence fixture is rejected (empty_property_required) and the not_property_required skip fixture is accepted; the task-062 property artifact is contract-valid; and the whole task-044 fixture suite (5 valid accepted, 8 invalid rejected) round-trips through the validator. Integrated property evidence with the pipeline artifacts: artifacts/pipeline/062/property/report.json carries real generated evidence (seeded_generator_determinism over 256 cases and forall_counterexample_reproducibility over 64 cases, both Determinism, both passing) and artifacts/pipeline/062/verification/report.json records the passing property stage. docs/PROPERTY_TEST_POLICY.md and docs/VERIFICATION_PIPELINE.md were corrected to state that task 062 ships the deterministic generator plus the Zig structural validator as the enforced contract, and that zentinel.pipeline.property_report.v1 is validated in Zig rather than by a registered JSON Schema (so it is intentionally absent from docs/SCHEMA_REGISTRY.md, which is outside this task's scope).
